_comment: YOUR application pipeline — you own this file. verify.py merges it into roles.json each run; the bot never overwrites it. pinned_roles = manually-tracked applications (kept even when closed). overrides = auto-discovered roles you've applied to (attaches pinned + app stage).

pinned_roles.role: Director of Data Science, Performance Marketing
pinned_roles.note: YOUR #1 ACTIVE ROLE and arguably the best of the whole search. Director title = the scope step-up you want, and the mandate is a bullseye on your wedge: lead the DS team running Root's performance-marketing engine — owning a NINE-FIGURE marketing budget, LTV-based bidding, incrementality/MMM measurement, experimentation. This is monetization + growth + experimentation + 0→1 leadership, exactly your story. Warm INBOUND from the hiring manager (Annie Yang sourced you on LinkedIn), not a cold app — a strong signal. Honest caveats: (1) company trajectory — Root is a small-cap public insurtech, but it just turned profitable (sub-100% combined ratio, record Q1'26), so the story is improving; (2) JD says PhD-preferred + full-funnel paid-acquisition experience — manage that gap by leaning on quant depth + actuarial-science degree + experimentation/monetization work. Frame everything as marketing spend → LTV-positive (profitable-risk) policies, not raw volume.

status.v: In process · HM round done 7/9 · awaiting outcome (nudge Kristin ~7/20)

app.note: INTERVIEW DONE 7/9: completed the 60-min HM technical/discussion round with Annie Yang (Sr. Director, DS Marketing). As of 7/20 no next-round word (~11 days silent, past the 7/16 self-nudge date). ACTION NOW: send Kristin Simmons a short, warm status nudge reiterating strong interest. Still your #1 active role and best of the search (Director title + wedge-perfect: LTV bidding, incrementality/MMM, experimentation on a nine-figure budget). Keep framing spend → LTV-positive policies, not raw volume.

pinned_roles.tier: Public e-commerce giant (NYSE: CPNG, ~$34.5B rev) · Coupang Ads
pinned_roles.note: REACTIVATED & HOT (7/20). What looked like a generic 'Director of DS' is really a DIRECTOR, ADS ANALYTICS role inside Coupang Ads — owning the financial goals of the ads product line: auction/monetization models, ad revenue & inventory forecasting, rigorous A/B testing, and advertiser adoption/churn KPIs across e-commerce + the Eats app. That is a WEDGE BULLSEYE for your monetization + experimentation + 0→1 story (auction = a monetization mechanism; the leadership problem is the same one you ran with TurboTax pricing and 5DE lending). Strategic tailwind: as Coupang's core retail margins compress (net loss in Q1'26), retail-media/ads is the highest-margin lever in the P&L — this org is a profit engine, not a cost center. HM = Siddharth Gupta (Director II, DS & Analytics, AdTech; ex-Meta / NerdWallet Head of Marketing Analytics / eBay — path overlaps yours). Domain gap = ad-tech/auctions; bridge it, don't hide it. See the prep cockpit: Coupang_HM_Prep_SiddharthGupta.html.

pinned_roles.tier: Consumer fintech · subsidiary of Upbound Group (NASDAQ: UPBD)
pinned_roles.note: NEW FIND (LinkedIn sweep 7/8). Director of DS at Brigit — a consumer financial-health app (instant cash advances, credit-building, subscription) that Upbound Group (NASDAQ: UPBD, ex-Rent-A-Center) acquired Jan 2025 for up to $460M. Brigit is GROWING and profitable-adjacent: ~1M+ paying subscribers, ~$215–230M rev in 2025, guided to ~$350–400M in 2026, now backed by a public parent (stability + resources). WEDGE BULLSEYE: consumer fintech, subscription monetization, credit/lending, customer-lifecycle + growth — exactly your story. Director title = the scope step-up you want, and an SF role for an NYC-HQ company likely means building/leading a new West-Coast DS footprint (0→1 team-building, your strength). CAVEATS: (1) it's now a subsidiary of Upbound — some prestige discount vs a pure venture fintech, though the financials are stronger for it; (2) apps are managed off LinkedIn, and there's a $60M 2026 earnout on Brigit's performance, so the org is under real growth pressure — frame yourself as the monetization/growth leader who hits those numbers.
